title: Oxymeris lindeniana
intro: Oxymeris lindeniana, also known as Tilia lindeniana or Lindens, is a deciduous tree species native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from stress, anxiety, and insomnia due to its sedative effects, as well as exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that aid in soothing respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The bioactive constituents of Oxymeris lindeniana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Oxymeris lindeniana include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used as a calming agent or to promote relaxation.
